---
title: FreeCampus Python
description: A beginner-first Python course from zero programming experience to practical projects.
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-kicker}
Beginner-first Python course
:::

# Learn Python from zero

Build practical confidence with short explanations, runnable examples, quizzes,
debugging practice, diagrams, notebooks, and projects. Start in the browser with
Google Colab, then grow into VS Code, Jupyter, testing, data science, and AI
libraries.

::: {.hero-actions}
[Start the course](lessons/getting-started/what-is-programming.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[View the lesson path](lessons/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg}
[Debug Python errors](lessons/debugging/error-messages.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-lg}
:::

::: {.hero-stats}
::: {.hero-stat}
**14**
chapters
:::

::: {.hero-stat}
**55+**
course pages
:::

::: {.hero-stat}
**OJS**
interactive quizzes
:::
:::
:::

::: {.course-grid .audience-grid}
::: {.course-card}
### New to programming?

Start with what programs are, how Python runs instructions, how notebooks work,
and why errors are feedback instead of failure.

[Begin with lesson 1](lessons/getting-started/what-is-programming.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.course-card}
### Already know some basics?

Jump to the parts you need: lists, dictionaries, functions, files, testing,
scientific Python, data science, or machine learning.

[Browse the full path](lessons/index.qmd){.card-link}
:::

::: {.course-card}
### Teaching this course?

Use learner profiles, instructor notes, section quizzes, debugging routines, and
small practice loops for live or self-paced instruction.

[Open instructor notes](lessons/instructor-notes.qmd){.card-link}
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip title="Course promise"}
You will not learn by memorizing commands. You will learn by predicting, running,
explaining, modifying, quizzing, and debugging small examples until the ideas are
usable.
:::

## How the course works

Each lesson follows a practical beginner loop:

1. read the purpose of the concept;
2. predict what a small example will do;
3. run it in Colab, Jupyter, VS Code, or the terminal;
4. answer one or more quick quizzes;
5. explain the result in everyday language;
6. change one small thing;
7. debug using clues instead of guessing.

::: {.course-grid}
::: {.course-card}
### Practice immediately

Use [Google Colab](lessons/getting-started/google-colab.qmd) to run Python in a
browser before installing anything.
:::

::: {.course-card}
### Build local confidence

Use [Local Python Tools](lessons/getting-started/local-python-tools.qmd) to set
up Python, VS Code, Jupyter, and the terminal.
:::

::: {.course-card}
### Keep errors calm

Keep [Error Messages](lessons/debugging/error-messages.qmd) nearby whenever
Python prints red text.
:::
:::

## Learning path

::: {.pathway}
1. [Getting Started](lessons/getting-started/index.qmd)
2. [Core Python](lessons/core-python/index.qmd)
3. [Data Structures](lessons/data-structures/index.qmd)
4. [Functions](lessons/functions/index.qmd)
5. [Debugging](lessons/debugging/index.qmd)
6. [Files and Data](lessons/files-and-data/index.qmd)
7. [Projects and Environments](lessons/projects-and-environments/index.qmd)
8. [Code Quality](lessons/code-quality/index.qmd)
9. [Object-Oriented Python](lessons/object-oriented-python/index.qmd)
10. [Advanced Python Patterns](lessons/advanced-python-patterns/index.qmd)
11. [Scientific Python](lessons/scientific-python/index.qmd)
12. [Data Science](lessons/data-science/index.qmd)
13. [Machine Learning and AI](lessons/machine-learning-ai/index.qmd)
14. [Capstone](lessons/capstone/index.qmd)
:::

## Tools you will meet

- [Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/) for no-install practice.
- [Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/) for local programming.
- [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) for project work.
- [Jupyter](https://jupyter.org/) for notebooks and data exploration.
- `ruff`, `pytest`, type hints, and CI for code quality.
- NumPy, pandas, Matplotlib, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow/Keras, and
  Transformers for applied work.

::: {.callout-note title="Not sure where to start?"}
If programming is new to you, start with [What Is Programming?](lessons/getting-started/what-is-programming.qmd).
If you have written small Python scripts before, skim the [lesson path](lessons/index.qmd)
and use the quizzes to find your level.
:::
